Name: UWidgetInteractionComponent
Description:
This is a component to allow interaction with the Widget Component.  This class allows you to 
  simulate a sort of laser pointer device, when it hovers over widgets it will send the basic signals 
  to show as if the mouse were moving on top of it.  You'll then tell the component to simulate key presses, 
  like Left Mouse, down and up, to simulate a mouse click.

Variables.Name: VirtualUserIndex
Variables.Description:
Represents the Virtual User Index.  Each virtual user should be represented by a different 
	  index number, this will maintain separate capture and focus states for them.  Each
	  controller or finger-tip should get a unique PointerIndex.

Variables.Name: PointerIndex
Variables.Description: Each user virtual controller or virtual finger tips being simulated should use a different pointer index.

Variables.Name: TraceChannel
Variables.Description: The trace channel to use when tracing for widget components in the world.

Variables.Name: InteractionDistance
Variables.Description: The distance in game units the component should be able to interact with a widget component.

Variables.Name: InteractionSource
Variables.Description:
Should we project from the world location of the component?  If you set this to false, you'll
	  need to call SetCustomHitResult(), and provide the result of a custom hit test form whatever
	  location you wish.

Variables.Name: bEnableHitTesting
Variables.Description:
Should the interaction component perform hit testing (Automatic or Custom) and attempt to 
	  simulate hover - if you were going to emulate a keyboard you would want to turn this option off
	  if the virtual keyboard was separate from the virtual pointer device and used a second interaction
	  component.

Variables.Name: bShowDebug
Variables.Description: Shows some debugging lines and a hit sphere to help you debug interactions.

Variables.Name: DebugColor
Variables.Description: Determines the color of the debug lines.

Variables.Name: CustomHitResult
Variables.Description: Stores the custom hit result set by the player.

Variables.Name: LocalHitLocation
Variables.Description: The 2D location on the widget component that was hit.

Variables.Name: LastLocalHitLocation
Variables.Description: The last 2D location on the widget component that was hit.

Variables.Name: HoveredWidgetComponent
Variables.Description: The widget component we're currently hovering over.

Variables.Name: LastHitResult
Variables.Description: The last hit result we used.

Variables.Name: bIsHoveredWidgetInteractable
Variables.Description: Are we hovering over any interactive widgets.

Variables.Name: bIsHoveredWidgetFocusable
Variables.Description: Are we hovering over any focusable widget?

Variables.Name: bIsHoveredWidgetHitTestVisible
Variables.Description: Are we hovered over a widget that is hit test visible?

Functions.Name: PressPointerKey
Functions.Description:
Presses a key as if the mousepointer were the source of it.  Normally you would just use
	  LeftRight mouse button for the Key.  However - advanced uses could also be imagined where you
	  send other keys to signal widgets to take special actions if they're under the cursor.

Functions.Name: ReleasePointerKey
Functions.Description:
Releases a key as if the mousepointer were the source of it.  Normally you would just use
	  LeftRight mouse button for the Key.  However - advanced uses could also be imagined where you
	  send other keys to signal widgets to take special actions if they're under the cursor.

Functions.Name: PressKey
Functions.Description:
Press a key as if it had come from the keyboard.  Avoid using this for 'a-z|A-Z', things like
	  the Editable Textbox in Slate expect OnKeyChar to be called to signal a specific character being
	  send to the widget.  So for those cases you should use SendKeyChar.

Functions.Name: ReleaseKey
Functions.Description: Releases a key as if it had been released by the keyboard.

Functions.Name: PressAndReleaseKey
Functions.Description: Does both the press and release of a simulated keyboard key.

Functions.Name: SendKeyChar
Functions.Description:
Transmits a list of characters to a widget by simulating a OnKeyChar event for each key listed in
	  the string.

Functions.Name: ScrollWheel
Functions.Description: Sends a scroll wheel event to the widget under the last hit result.

Functions.Name: GetHoveredWidgetComponent
Functions.Description: Get the currently hovered widget component.

Functions.Name: IsOverInteractableWidget
Functions.Description:
Returns true if a widget under the hit result is interactive.  e.g. Slate widgets 
	  that return true for IsInteractable().

Functions.Name: IsOverFocusableWidget
Functions.Description:
Returns true if a widget under the hit result is focusable.  e.g. Slate widgets that 
	  return true for SupportsKeyboardFocus().

Functions.Name: IsOverHitTestVisibleWidget
Functions.Description:
Returns true if a widget under the hit result is has a visibility that makes it hit test 
	  visible.  e.g. Slate widgets that return true for GetVisibility().IsHitTestVisible().

Functions.Name: GetLastHitResult
Functions.Description: Gets the last hit result generated by the component.  Returns the custom hit result if that was set.

Functions.Name: Get2DHitLocation
Functions.Description: Gets the last hit location on the widget in 2D, local pixel units of the render target.

Functions.Name: SetCustomHitResult
Functions.Description: Set custom hit result.  This is only taken into account if InteractionSource is set to EWidgetInteractionSource::Custom.

Delegate.Name: OnHoveredWidgetChanged
Delegate.Description:
Called when the hovered Widget Component changes.  The interaction component functions at the Slate
	  level - so it's unable to report anything about what UWidget is under the hit result.
